CheckTitle: AI agent policies must not grant overprivileged access
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Permissions**. Verifies that no AI agent identity has policies containing wildcard actions (e.g. `s3:*`, `*:*`) or an attached AWS-managed `AdministratorAccess` / `PowerUserAccess` policy. Either condition grants permissions far beyond what the agent's declared purpose requires, violating the principle of least privilege.
Risk: An overprivileged agent can read, write, or delete any resource in scope of its wildcard or admin policy. A single prompt-injection, supply-chain compromise, or credential theft can escalate into a full account takeover or mass data exfiltration.

Code.Other:
1. List all policies attached to the agent role: `aws iam list-attached-role-policies --role-name <role>`.
2. Identify any policy with Action: '*' or Action: '<service>:*' or the AdministratorAccess managed policy.
3. Create a new customer-managed policy with only the specific actions and resources required.
4. Detach the overprivileged policy and attach the scoped replacement.
5. Run `aws iam simulate-principal-policy` to verify the new policy grants only intended access.
6. Re-test the agent's functionality to confirm no legitimate operations are broken.

Recommendation.Text: Replace wildcard and admin policies with customer-managed policies that enumerate only the specific IAM actions and resource ARNs required by the agent. Use IAM Access Analyzer policy generation to derive a minimal policy from CloudTrail activity. Review and tighten the policy quarterly.

Notes: ASPM-013 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
